page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 680.61 GB/sec for cached small blocks and approximately 51.52 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

While the instance delivers average performance for database operations, static web serving, and small-scale LLM inference, it exhibits weaker performance in text generation for larger models, such as the 7B parameter model which averages 7.62 tokens per second. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads that benefit from modern DDR5 memory and Intel AMX instructions without requiring continuous peak compute. It is suited for applications such as web serving, text processing, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression benchmarks, while maintaining average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance represents a balanced, qualitatively cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C CPU, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring DDR5 memory, Intel AMX support, and strong single-core performance for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set extensions
Delivers strong single-core performance in text processing and compression
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps without local storage
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose applications
